title: Define persistence, rebuild, and issuance responsibilities
goal: Translate the plan-defined materialized-state contract, event-log role, issuance metadata role, write-order guarantees, rebuild order, and malformed-vs-missing behavior into a fully traceable implementation-facing specification.
scope: Define the canonical role of tx_state.json exactly as written in plan.md.
Define the canonical role of tx_event_log.jsonl exactly as written in plan.md.
Define the canonical role and required fields of tx_id_counter.json exactly as written in plan.md.
Define the write-order guarantees, rebuild fallback order, and malformed-versus-missing behavior exactly as written in plan.md.
out_of_scope: Adding extra canonical persistence artifacts not defined in plan.md.
Redefining historical compatibility beyond bounded behavior described in plan.md.
expected_outputs: A persistence-contract specification derived strictly from plan.md.
A rebuild and durability specification derived strictly from plan.md.
A traceability matrix linking persistence requirements to exact plan sections and runtime review targets.
acceptance_criteria: Materialized-state requirements match the top-level minimal continuation contract described in plan.md.
The event-log role is described as authoritative history and rebuild source without displacing materialized state as the primary healthy resume entrypoint.
The issuance metadata contract preserves integer tx_id issuance semantics and clear malformed-file failure behavior.
The artifact does not invent additional canonical persistence behavior beyond plan.md.

forbidden_moves: Do not treat planning artifacts as canonical runtime state.
Do not permit malformed canonical persistence to degrade silently into healthy baseline behavior.
